name: CRUD/patch-fhirpath
description: FHIRPath Patch (application/fhir+json PATCH with a Parameters resource carrying 'patch-op' operations, per http://hl7.org/fhir/fhirpatch.html) conformance tests, adapted from the Microsoft FHIR Server E2E suite FhirPathPatchTests.cs. Covers add/replace/delete operations, FHIRPath path filters (where()), choice-type handling, immutable-element protection, conditional (If-Match/ETag) semantics, and date/dateTime edge cases. Bundle-transaction patch scenarios and the SQL-Server-only history-suppression assertion are noted inline; see the top-level suite summary for what is intentionally out of scope.

test.name: replace and delete operations patch correctly
test.description: A single FHIRPath Patch request with a 'replace' on Patient.gender and a 'delete' on Patient.address must succeed with 200 and apply both edits.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Replace gender to female and delete the address collection

assert.description: Patch must return HTTP 200 OK
Resource body must be a Patient
gender must now be 'female'
address must have been removed

test.name: unknown patch operation type is rejected
test.description: Submitting a patch operation whose 'type' part is not one of add/insert/delete/replace/upsert/move must be rejected with 400 and a diagnostic naming the allowed types.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Submit a patch operation with type 'coo', which is not a recognized FHIRPath Patch operation

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request for an unrecognized operation type
Diagnostics must name the invalid operation type and the allowed set
assert.value: Invalid patch operation type: 'coo'. Only 'add', 'insert', 'delete', 'replace', 'upsert', and 'move' are allowed.

test.name: replace operation without a path is rejected
test.description: A 'replace' patch operation that omits the required 'path' part must be rejected with 400.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Submit a 'replace' operation with no 'path' part

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request
Diagnostics must state that replace requires a path
assert.value: Patch replace operations must have the 'path' part.

test.name: replace operation without a value is rejected
test.description: A 'replace' patch operation that has a 'path' but omits the required 'value' part must be rejected with 400.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Submit a 'replace' operation with a path but no value

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request
Diagnostics must state that replace requires a value
assert.value: Patch replace operations must have the 'value' part.

test.name: add operation accepts a complex object value
test.description: An 'add' patch operation whose value is a complex type (Address) must be applied, resulting in a single address entry.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-no-address' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201
Fixture patient must start with no address

operation.description: Add a complete Address object to the empty address collection

assert.description: Patch must return HTTP 200 OK
exactly one address must now be present

test.name: replace operation supports FHIRPath where() filters
test.description: A 'replace' patch path may use a FHIRPath where() filter to target a specific repeating element, here replacing the city of the address whose use is 'home'.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Replace the city of the address selected via where(use = 'home')

assert.description: Patch must return HTTP 200 OK
exactly one address must remain
city must now be 'Portland'

test.name: add operation on a nonexistent element is rejected
test.description: An 'add' patch operation naming a property that does not exist on the target type must be rejected with 400 and a diagnostic naming the element.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Attempt to add a property named 'dummyProperty', which does not exist on Patient

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request
Diagnostics must name the missing element
assert.value: Element dummyProperty not found at Patient when processing patch add operation.

test.name: replace operation on an immutable element is rejected
test.description: A 'replace' patch operation targeting a server-controlled/immutable element (id, meta.versionId, text.div, text.status are all equally forbidden) must be rejected with 400. Patient.id is used as the representative case.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Attempt to replace the server-assigned Patient.id

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request
Diagnostics must mention that the element is immutable

test.name: replace operation with an invalid typed value is rejected
test.description: A 'replace' patch operation whose value cannot be coerced to the target element's type (here an arbitrary string for the coded 'gender' element; birthDate and address.use with invalid values behave identically) must be rejected with 400 and a diagnostic naming the element and operation.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Attempt to replace gender with a value that is not a valid administrative-gender code

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request
Diagnostics must state the invalid input for gender
assert.value: Invalid input for gender at Patient.gender when processing patch replace operation.

test.name: patching a nonexistent resource returns 404
test.description: Submitting a FHIRPath Patch for a Patient id that was never created must return 404 Not Found.

operation.description: Attempt to patch a Patient id that does not exist on the server

assert.description: Server must return 404 Not Found

test.name: add operation on an already-populated element is rejected
test.description: An 'add' patch operation targeting a choice-type element (deceased[x]) that already has a value must be rejected with 400, since 'add' requires the element to be absent; 'replace' must be used instead.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201
Fixture patient must already have a deceased value

operation.description: Attempt to 'add' a deceasedDateTime even though deceasedBoolean already exists

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request
Diagnostics must mention the existing deceased element

test.name: replace operation changes the type of an existing choice element
test.description: A 'replace' patch operation on a choice-type element (Patient.deceased) may switch both the value and the concrete type (deceasedBoolean -> deceasedDateTime) in one step, and must succeed with 200.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Replace deceasedBoolean with a deceasedDateTime value

assert.description: Patch must return HTTP 200 OK
deceased must now carry a dateTime value

test.name: patch with a stale If-Match version fails with 412
test.description: Submitting a patch with an If-Match header referencing a version that does not match the resource's current version must be rejected with 412 Precondition Failed.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Submit a patch with If-Match: W/"5" against a resource that is still at version 1

assert.description: Server must return 412 Precondition Failed

test.name: patch with a matching If-Match version succeeds
test.description: Submitting a patch with an If-Match header that matches the resource's current version must succeed with 200.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Submit a patch with If-Match: W/"1" against a freshly created (version 1) resource

assert.description: Patch must return HTTP 200 OK

test.name: malformed nested backbone value is rejected
test.description: An 'add' operation whose value is built from two anonymous 'value' parts (instead of the named sub-element parts a BackboneElement like Patient.link expects) must be rejected with 400.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Attempt to add a Patient.link entry using two unnamed 'value' parts instead of named sub-parts

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request
Diagnostics must indicate an element was not found while resolving the malformed part

test.name: add operation accepts a BackboneElement value with named sub-parts
test.description: An 'add' operation whose value is built from correctly named sub-parts ('type' and 'other') for the Patient.link BackboneElement must succeed with 200.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Add a Patient.link entry using correctly named 'type'/'other' sub-parts

assert.description: Patch must return HTTP 200 OK

test.name: dateTime value with a timezone offset is preserved
test.description: An 'add' patch operation whose value is a dateTime with a non-zero timezone offset must preserve that offset in the stored and returned value.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-minimal' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Add a deceasedDateTime of 2022-05-02T14:00:00+02:00 to a patient with no prior deceased value

assert.description: Patch must return HTTP 200 OK
the returned dateTime must retain the +02:00 offset

test.name: add operation on a Date element parses a date-only value
test.description: An 'add' patch operation whose value is a bare date (no time component) applied to a FHIR 'date' typed element (ActivityDefinition.approvalDate) must succeed and round-trip exactly.

operation.description: Create a fresh ActivityDefinition from fixture 'activitydefinition-base' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Add approvalDate = 2022-05-02 (date, no time)

assert.description: Patch must return HTTP 200 OK
approvalDate must equal the supplied date

test.name: add operation rejects a dateTime value for a Date element
test.description: A FHIR 'date' element (ActivityDefinition.approvalDate) may not contain time information. Submitting a valueDateTime (with or without an offset) for that path must be rejected with 400 so the resource never enters an invalid state.

operation.description: Create a fresh ActivityDefinition from fixture 'activitydefinition-base' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Attempt to add approvalDate = 2022-05-02T14:00:00 (a dateTime, not a date)

assert.description: Server must return 400 Bad Request rather than accept time information on a Date element

test.name: _meta-history=false suppresses a new history entry
test.description: Some data stores (notably SQL Server) honor a '_meta-history=false' query parameter on patch by applying the change without recording a new version in the resource's history. This is warningOnly because not every supported data store implements the optimization.

operation.description: Create a fresh Patient from fixture 'patient-full' to patch in this test

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201

operation.description: Patch with _meta-history=false

assert.description: Patch must still return HTTP 200 OK regardless of history handling

operation.description: Read the full history of the patched Patient

assert.description: History response must be a Bundle
Only the original create should remain in history when _meta-history=false is honored
